page: Amazon Web Services x8g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It does not feature any local storage or bundled GPUs, and relies on the Nitro hypervisor. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top tier (top 10%). Memory bandwidth tests show 924.86 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 154.74 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-threaded performance is strong in specific tasks, such as gzip compression (30.68 GB/sec) and text processing (2349.0 on Geekbench). However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the weak category (bottom 25%). Additionally, LLM inference tasks show mixed results, with text generation on a small 135M model scoring as weak and prompt processing on a medium 7B model scoring as poor.

Given its high memory density of 16.0 GB per vCPU, the x8g.2xlarge offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-performance cryptography. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, including database operations, caching layers like Redis (which achieved 18.31 million operations per second in testing), and memory-heavy text processing or software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB per core, while lacking local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores and strong single-threaded compression and text processing capabilities. However, cryptographic performance and LLM inference speeds represent notable performance tradeoffs. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, making it highly suitable for database operations, caching systems like Redis, and memory-intensive software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory performance, ideal for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.

Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory performance and high memory bandwidth
